name: Burgoo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-quart Dutch oven or stock pot, combine chicken, beef cross-cuts, water, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook until the meat is tender, about 1 hour.
2. Remove chicken and beef from the broth, reserving the broth. Remove chicken and beef from bones; discard skin and bones. Cube the beef and chicken. Set aside.
3. Cook bacon in the pot until crisp; drain, then crumble it and set aside.
4. To the reserved broth in the Dutch oven, add cubed beef, undrained tomatoes, potatoes, carrots, onion, celery, green pepper, sugar, red pepper, cloves, garlic, and bay leaf. Cover and simmer for 1 hour, stirring often.
5. Remove cloves and bay leaf. Using a knife, make cuts down the center of each row of corn kernels and scrape off the cobs. Add corn, cubed chicken, undrained beans, and okra to the Dutch oven; simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Blend flour with reserved bacon drippings and stir into the stew. Cook until the stew thickens. Season with salt to taste. Garnish with parsley and serve hot with baking powder biscuits for a great meal.
